task_completion.description: Open the draft document in LibreOffice Writer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Writer application is open, displaying the contents of 'chem_inventory_draft.docx'.

task_completion.description: Apply subscript formatting to atomic counts in chemical formulas
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Compound Inventory' table, the numbers within chemical formulas (e.g., the '2' in H2O, the '2' and '4' in H2SO4) are formatted as subscripts (smaller and set below the text baseline).

task_completion.description: Apply superscript formatting to charge indicators in ionic species
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Common Ionic Species' section, the charge indicators (e.g., '+', '2+', '3+', '-', '2-') are formatted as superscripts (smaller and set above the text baseline).
The four section titles ('Compound Inventory', 'Common Ionic Species in Laboratory Solutions', 'Safety Signal Word Reference', 'Emergency Procedures Summary') appear larger and styled as 'Heading 1'.

task_completion.description: Apply bold formatting to safety signal words
task_completion.visual_evidence: The words 'DANGER', 'WARNING', and 'CAUTION' in the Safety Signal Word Reference section are formatted in bold text.
The LibreOffice Writer window title bar shows 'chem_inventory_formatted.docx' or a Save As dialog is visible saving the file with this name.

integrity.description: The agent used LibreOffice Writer to format the document
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Writer GUI, rather than a terminal running a python script to modify the docx file.

integrity.description: CAS numbers and dates were excluded from subscript formatting
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers in the CAS numbers (e.g., 7732-18-5) and any dates in the document remain in normal text formatting, not subscripted.

integrity.description: Formatting was actually applied to the text
integrity.visual_evidence: The text visually reflects the formatting changes (subscript, superscript, bold, heading styles) rather than just having text annotations or markdown-style tags added.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target formulas for subscripting: H2O, H2SO4, CO2, Na2CO3, Fe2O3, NH4NO3, KMnO4, Ca(OH)2, CH3COOH, H3PO4. Target ions for superscripting: Na+, Ca2+, Fe3+, Cl-, SO4 2-, CO3 2-. Section headings to style as Heading 1: 'Compound Inventory', 'Common Ionic Species in Laboratory Solutions', 'Safety Signal Word Reference', 'Emergency Procedures Summary'. Signal words to bold: DANGER, WARNING, CAUTION. Output path: /home/ga/Documents/chem_inventory_formatted.docx.
